notes: Mirrored central arena with a safe horizontal relic lane, fire-taxed vertical entries, and paired bushes immediately before the pickup. The hypothesis is that low-search policies overcommit to the obvious center race, while stronger MCTS can time ambushes, guards, and detours through the braided upper/lower approaches without creating a wall maze.
conclusion: This map produced a much stronger score than baseline: 56.63 versus 12.57, driven by perfect measured side fairness, full horizon credit, no turn caps, and large separation between the higher MCTS rungs. The hypothesis mostly held: greedy and low-search were nearly flat, but mid and high search found much better play, with low->mid and mid->high adjacent Elo gaps both above 335. The main weakness is still speed, since median game length was only 9 turns and the length penalty reduced the final score to 75% of its otherwise eligible value. The surprise was that adding central ambush bushes and vertical fire gates improved fairness to a 0.500 blue mirror winrate without increasing replay risk. A concrete next experiment would keep this central bush/fire mechanism but move bases or starts one tile farther from the relic path to target median length >= 12 without flattening the high-rung Elo gap.
